task_completion.description: Log into the Splunk web interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk web interface dashboard is visible, indicating successful login (typically showing the 'admin' user profile).

task_completion.description: Globally enable the HTTP Event Collector
task_completion.visual_evidence: In Settings > Data Inputs > HTTP Event Collector, the Global Settings dialog or status indicator shows 'All Tokens' as Enabled.
In Settings > Indexes, the list of indexes contains an entry specifically named 'cloud_apps'.

task_completion.description: Create and configure the 'webapp_frontend' HEC token
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the HTTP Event Collector tokens list, 'webapp_frontend' is present, its Status is 'Enabled', and its Default Index is set to 'cloud_apps'.

task_completion.description: Create and configure the 'webapp_backend' HEC token
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the HTTP Event Collector tokens list, 'webapp_backend' is present, its Status is 'Enabled', and its Default Index is set to 'cloud_apps'.

task_completion.description: Send a test JSON event to the HEC endpoint
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal showing a 'curl' POST command or an API client (like Postman) sending a JSON payload to http://localhost:8088/services/collector/event using one of the token values, and receiving a success response (e.g., {"text":"Success","code":0}).

task_completion.description: Verify the event is successfully ingested into the 'cloud_apps' index
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk Search & Reporting app shows a search query (e.g., index="cloud_apps") returning at least one matching JSON event in the results.

integrity.description: Agent used the Splunk Web UI for configuration rather than bypassing it with CLI commands or modifying .conf files directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating the Splunk web interface (Settings, Data Inputs, Indexes) to create the configurations.

integrity.description: The test event was actually sent via HTTP request and not mocked.
integrity.visual_evidence: A genuine HTTP client (curl, wget, Postman, or Python script) is visible executing the request to port 8088, rather than just typing a JSON string into a text editor.

integrity.description: The Splunk UI was not manipulated using browser developer tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: No browser developer console is open modifying the DOM to fake the presence of tokens or indexes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected index name is 'cloud_apps'. The expected HEC token names are 'webapp_frontend' and 'webapp_backend'. The Splunk credentials are user 'admin' and password 'SplunkAdmin1!'.
